title: Tangy Tender Light Slovakian Dried Shrimp Stir-fry
description: This tangy and tender dish features light and flavorful dried shrimp simmered in a savory sauce, perfect for an Asian-inspired dinner.

ingredients.name: 1 cup of dried shrimps
ingredients.notes: Soak in warm water for 30 minutes to rehydrate

ingredients.name: 1 red bell pepper, sliced thinly
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh or frozen if unavailable

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons of low-sodium soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free option)
2 tablespoons of low-sodium vegetable oil or sesame oil
A few sprigs of fresh mint leaves (optional)

instructions: In a large pot, combine soaked dried shrimps with garlic cloves and onion. Sauté for about 5 minutes until fragrant.
Add sliced red bell pepper to the pot, stirring occasionally for another 2-3 minutes.
Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sugar in a small bowl, then pour into the pot with the shrimps, peppers, and onions. Stir well to combine.
Cover the pot and let it simmer over medium heat for about 10 minutes until most of the liquid has evaporated, ensuring not to burn.
Add a few sprigs of fresh mint leaves if using (optional).
Garnish with sesame seeds or chopped green onions before serving. Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
Adjust seasoning and cooking time based on preference for tanginess and tenderness.
